title: elymus macrourus subsp. turuchanensis
intro: Elymus macrourus subsp. turuchanensis is a subspecies of the Elymus macrourus plant, commonly known as a type of grass, specifically belonging to the family Poaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Russia, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Elymus macrourus subsp. turuchanensis include its ability to treat various conditions such as kidney stones, urinary tract infections, and edema, as well as its potential to reduce blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
